suggested_topics: the engineering of ancient Roman aqueducts
how mycelium networks share resources underground
the economics of food trucks in major cities
the role of smell in human memory formation

formats.name: micro_manifesto
formats.instruction: Write a punchy declaration for something you believe in—just one or two sentences, each starting with 'I stand for...' or 'I refuse to...'. Make it feel like a mini protest sign.

formats.name: echoes_and_reverberations
formats.instruction: Recall the very first time you encountered a concept or object, then describe how it shaped or haunted you over time. Minimum three mini-epochs, each separated by a line break.

formats.name: reverse_advice
formats.instruction: Reflect on a piece of advice you once received and make a case for why ignoring it actually improved your life. Close with a pithy one-liner.

formats.name: snapshot_scene
formats.instruction: Paint a vivid picture of a single moment in time. Focus on sensory details and atmosphere—no backstory, no explanation. Like a photograph made with words.

formats.name: future_flash
formats.instruction: Imagine a day in the future—describe it as if it’s happening now. Use present tense. Highlight how things have changed. Begin with 'It’s the year [XXXX] and…'.

formats.name: contrarian_catalog
formats.instruction: List three to five wildly unconventional beliefs or practices related to your topic. Start each with 'I believe...' or 'I do...'. Summarize the shock value in one closing sentence.

formats.name: deep_dive_dissection
formats.instruction: Take a single concept, trend, or object. Break it down layer by layer—history, current relevance, hidden quirks, surprising impacts. Each section must begin with a rhetorical question.

formats.name: poetic_infobox
formats.instruction: Write four lines, each a standalone poetic fact or observation about the topic. No need to rhyme—just use rich imagery, metaphor, or unexpected truth.

formats.name: debate_duet
formats.instruction: Write a mini-dialogue between two personas—one pro, one con—about a topic. Each gets two turns, no more than two sentences per turn. End with a surprise twist.

title_styles: Write a poetic metaphor title. Examples: 'X is a lighthouse in a storm', 'Why Y is a garden growing wild', 'Z: a bridge we keep crossing'
Write a provocative question. Examples: 'Is X just nostalgia in disguise?', 'Should we abandon Y forever?', 'Are we blinded by Z?'
Write a timeline teaser. Examples: 'From X to Z: My evolving journey', 'The rise and fall of Y', 'How Z changed my world then—and now'
Write an imaginary headline. Examples: 'Breaking: X overturns Y', 'In the year 2040, Z is king', 'Scientists prove Y never existed'
Write a forbidden confession. Examples: 'I secretly love X', 'Here’s why I never trusted Y', 'Z, and why I can’t live without it'
structure_variants: Write as a rapid-fire dialogue: alternate between two voices for every point.
Organize your post as if it’s a recipe—ingredients, steps, taste test, cleanup.
Use a stream-of-consciousness style—no punctuation, just thoughts ebbing and flowing.
Break your post into three contrasting moods: hopeful, cynical, amused.
Format as a series of diary entries from different days or times.
topic_angles: Explore moments when technology failed you at the worst possible time.
Reflect on the secret rituals or habits that shape your daily routine.
Investigate the myths and truths behind generational divides.
Analyze how pop culture distorts our expectations of real life.
Describe the emotional landscape of waiting for something to change.
